transcripts.title: Naive Player Playthrough
transcripts.description: A first-time IF player stumbles through the game using natural language

commands.output:
Guess the Verb
By John Googol

Type HELP for instructions.

Front Porch

A sagging wooden porch wraps around the front of the house. A woven doormat lies before the front door. A tarnished brass doorbell is set beside the frame.

The front door leads inside to the east.

You can see front door, woven doormat, brass doorbell, sagging porch here.
commands.comment:
# Front Porch — fumbling around
A naive player looks around first

commands.output: A faded doormat reading 'WELCOME'. One corner is curled up -- something glints underneath.
commands.comment: They try to examine things they see mentioned

commands.output: A tarnished brass button set into the door frame.
You press the doorbell. A faint chime echoes inside the house, unanswered.
commands.comment: A naive player tries to "ring" the doorbell — natural English

commands.output: You lift the corner of the doormat and find an iron key hidden underneath.
commands.comment: They try to take the mat — should reveal the key (not blank)

commands.output: You already found the key here.
commands.comment: They try lifting the mat again — key already revealed

commands.output: You already found the key here.
Taken.
commands.comment: They see the key and take it

commands.output: You unlock the front door with the iron key.
commands.comment: A naive player might try "use key on door"

commands.output: You open the front door.

Hallway

A dim hallway with faded wallpaper and creaking floorboards. A coat rack stands by the door with an old overcoat hanging from it.

Doors lead east to a study and south to the kitchen. A narrow staircase leads up.

You can see coat rack, old overcoat, narrow staircase, faded wallpaper, creaking floorboards here.
A moth-eaten wool overcoat. The pockets look like they might hold something.
commands.comment:
# Hallway — searching and climbing
They see the overcoat mentioned and try natural verbs

commands.output: You rummage through the overcoat's pockets and find a small book of matches from 'The Golden Lantern.'
commands.comment: "check pockets" is a very natural thing to try

commands.output: (Try going UP to climb the stairs.)
commands.comment: They see a staircase mentioned, try to climb it

commands.output:
Attic

A cramped attic under sloping eaves, thick with dust. A dusty shelf holds an old music box. A heavy steamer trunk sits against the wall.

The stairs lead back down.

You can see dusty shelf, steamer trunk, sloping eaves here.
commands.comment: The game hints to go UP, so the naive player tries that

commands.output:
Hallway

A dim hallway with faded wallpaper and creaking floorboards. A coat rack stands by the door with an old overcoat hanging from it.

Doors lead east to a study and south to the kitchen. A narrow staircase leads up.

You can see coat rack, old overcoat, narrow staircase, faded wallpaper, creaking floorboards here.
commands.comment:
# Study — fire and painting puzzles
They went up, now back down to continue exploring

commands.output:
Study

A wood-paneled study with built-in bookshelves. A heavy desk sits in the center, its drawers shut. An oil painting of a woman hangs on the far wall. A stone fireplace squats in the corner, cold and dark.

The hallway is back to the west.

You can see heavy desk, study bookshelves, oil painting, stone fireplace, hearthstone, wood paneling here.

You open the heavy desk.
Inside the heavy desk you see winding key.
commands.comment: They see a desk and try to open it

commands.output: You take the winding key from the heavy desk.
You push the painting aside, revealing a small wall safe hidden behind it.
commands.comment: They see a painting and try to look behind it

commands.output:
You strike a match and light the logs. The fire catches quickly, casting warm light across the study.

As the flames illuminate the hearthstone, you notice numbers scratched into the soot-blackened surface: 7 - 3 - 9.
commands.comment: They notice the fireplace and try to light it

commands.output: In the firelight, you can make out numbers scratched into the hearthstone: 7 - 3 - 9.
You dial 7... 3... 9. Click. The safe door swings open.
commands.comment: They know the combo now, try to open the safe

commands.output: You take the clock spring from the wall safe.

Hallway

A dim hallway with faded wallpaper and creaking floorboards. A coat rack stands by the door with an old overcoat hanging from it.

Doors lead east to a study and south to the kitchen. A narrow staircase leads up.

You can see coat rack, old overcoat, narrow staircase, faded wallpaper, creaking floorboards here.

Kitchen

A rustic kitchen with stone counters and a deep ceramic sink. Copper pots hang from hooks above. A handwritten recipe card sits on the counter.

The hallway is north. A doorway leads south to the garden.

You can see stone counters, ceramic sink, copper pots, recipe card here.

Garden

An overgrown garden behind the house. A flower bed runs along the back wall, thick with weeds. A small shed leans in one corner with its door ajar. A rusty trowel leans against the shed.

The kitchen doorway is back to the north.

You can see garden trowel, flower bed, garden shed here.
Taken.
You dig into the soft earth with the trowel and uncover a small brass mechanism -- gears, pins, and a tiny drum with raised bumps. The innards of a music box.
commands.comment: A naive player might just type "dig" with no target

commands.output: You've already dug here and found the mechanism.
commands.comment: Or they might try "dig flower bed"

commands.output: Taken.

Kitchen

A rustic kitchen with stone counters and a deep ceramic sink. Copper pots hang from hooks above. A handwritten recipe card sits on the counter.

The hallway is north. A doorway leads south to the garden.

You can see stone counters, ceramic sink, copper pots, recipe card here.

Hallway

A dim hallway with faded wallpaper and creaking floorboards. A coat rack stands by the door with an old overcoat hanging from it.

Doors lead east to a study and south to the kitchen. A narrow staircase leads up.

You can see coat rack, old overcoat, narrow staircase, faded wallpaper, creaking floorboards here.

Attic

A cramped attic under sloping eaves, thick with dust. A dusty shelf holds an old music box. A heavy steamer trunk sits against the wall.

The stairs lead back down.

You can see dusty shelf, steamer trunk, sloping eaves here.
A wooden music box with a rose-carved lid and a small keyhole on the side. Inside, the cavity is empty -- the mechanism has been removed.
commands.comment: They see the music box and try to fix it

commands.output: You take the old music box from the dusty shelf.
You put the brass mechanism in the old music box.
You put the clock spring in the old music box.

You insert the winding key and turn it gently. The music box comes alive -- a delicate melody fills the attic, tinkling and sweet.

As the last note fades, you hear a mechanical click from the steamer trunk -- its lock releases and the lid swings open.
Steamer trunk is already open.
commands.comment: The trunk opened! A naive player opens it

commands.output:
You take the family locket from the steamer trunk.
You found your grandmother's heirloom.
You lift the locket from the trunk. Inside, your grandmother smiles back at you from a faded photograph. This is what she wanted you to have.

transcripts.title: Unconventional Commands
transcripts.description: Commands that feel valid in English but aren't standard IF — tests disambiguation and parser coverage

commands.output:
Guess the Verb
By John Googol

Type HELP for instructions.

Front Porch

A sagging wooden porch wraps around the front of the house. A woven doormat lies before the front door. A tarnished brass doorbell is set beside the frame.

The front door leads inside to the east.

You can see front door, woven doormat, brass doorbell, sagging porch here.
commands.comment: # Front Porch — alternate verbs and nouns

commands.output: A faded doormat reading 'WELCOME'. One corner is curled up -- something glints underneath.
A faded doormat reading 'WELCOME'. One corner is curled up -- something glints underneath.
A tarnished brass button set into the door frame.
A tarnished brass button set into the door frame.
You rap your knuckles on the door. No answer.
I don't understand that.
commands.comment: "flip" the mat — physical manipulation (parser doesn't know "flip")

commands.output: A faded doormat reading 'WELCOME'. One corner is curled up -- something glints underneath.
You can't see any such thing.
commands.comment: Use-based phrasing (very common from non-IF players)

commands.output: You lift the corner of the doormat and find an iron key hidden underneath.
Taken.
How do you want to use front door? Try a specific verb: OPEN, PUSH, PULL, TURN, EAT, WEAR, etc.
You unlock the front door with the iron key.
You open the front door.

Hallway

A dim hallway with faded wallpaper and creaking floorboards. A coat rack stands by the door with an old overcoat hanging from it.

Doors lead east to a study and south to the kitchen. A narrow staircase leads up.

You can see coat rack, old overcoat, narrow staircase, faded wallpaper, creaking floorboards here.
A moth-eaten wool overcoat. The pockets look like they might hold something.
commands.comment:
# Hallway — alternate nouns and search phrasing
"coat" for "overcoat"

commands.output: A moth-eaten wool overcoat. The pockets look like they might hold something.
A moth-eaten wool overcoat. The pockets look like they might hold something.
You rummage through the overcoat's pockets and find a small book of matches from 'The Golden Lantern.'
You'll have to say which direction to go.
commands.comment:
Direction synonyms
"go upstairs" — not recognized as direction

commands.output: I don't understand that.

Study

A wood-paneled study with built-in bookshelves. A heavy desk sits in the center, its drawers shut. An oil painting of a woman hangs on the far wall. A stone fireplace squats in the corner, cold and dark.

The hallway is back to the west.

You can see heavy desk, study bookshelves, oil painting, stone fireplace, hearthstone, wood paneling here.
commands.comment: # Study — fire, painting, and safe puzzles

commands.output:
You open the heavy desk.
Inside the heavy desk you see winding key.
You take the winding key from the heavy desk.
A portrait of a stern woman in a high collar -- your grandmother, perhaps. The frame sits slightly askew on the wall.
A portrait of a stern woman in a high collar -- your grandmother, perhaps. The frame sits slightly askew on the wall.
You push the painting aside, revealing a small wall safe hidden behind it.
You already moved the painting. The wall safe is visible.

You strike a match and light the logs. The fire catches quickly, casting warm light across the study.

As the flames illuminate the hearthstone, you notice numbers scratched into the soot-blackened surface: 7 - 3 - 9.
The fire is already burning.
The fire is already burning.
The fireplace crackles with warm flames. The logs glow orange.
commands.comment:
Alternate nouns for hearthstone
"hearth" resolves to fireplace, not hearthstone

commands.output: Remembering the numbers on the hearthstone, you dial 7... 3... 9. Click. The safe door swings open.
commands.comment:
Try opening safe before combo — should say something about needing the combo
(Note: safe was already opened by dial, so "open safe" on already-open safe = blank — engine bug)

commands.output: You dial 7... 3... 9. Click. The safe door swings open.
You take the clock spring from the wall safe.

Hallway

A dim hallway with faded wallpaper and creaking floorboards. A coat rack stands by the door with an old overcoat hanging from it.

Doors lead east to a study and south to the kitchen. A narrow staircase leads up.

You can see coat rack, old overcoat, narrow staircase, faded wallpaper, creaking floorboards here.

Kitchen

A rustic kitchen with stone counters and a deep ceramic sink. Copper pots hang from hooks above. A handwritten recipe card sits on the counter.

The hallway is north. A doorway leads south to the garden.

You can see stone counters, ceramic sink, copper pots, recipe card here.
You turn the faucet. Rusty water sputters out briefly, then runs clear. Nothing else happens.

The recipe card reads:
Lavender shortbread -- butter, sugar, flour, and dried lavender from the garden.

Garden

An overgrown garden behind the house. A flower bed runs along the back wall, thick with weeds. A small shed leans in one corner with its door ajar. A rusty trowel leans against the shed.

The kitchen doorway is back to the north.

You can see garden trowel, flower bed, garden shed here.

You've already dug here and found the mechanism.

The stairs lead back down.
